confirmation_nudge_sent_at.description:
ConfirmationNudgeSentAt records when the one-time reminder to confirm
was sent, so an unconfirmed subscriber is never nudged more than once.

in_digest.description: true when the item is linked to a digest issue

original_url.description: listing on the source platform (e.g. HN comments page), when different from URL

position.description: ordering within a digest issue; 0 when not linked

url.description: click target — the external content the source is linking to

BearerAuth.description: Bearer token supplied in the Authorization header.
BearerAuth.name: Authorization

info.description: The GoDaily news digest API — ranked, summarised Go news delivered daily.
info.title: GoDaily API

get.description: Confirms a pending subscription using the token from the confirmation email, then redirects.

302.description: Redirect to /confirmed/ on success or / when the token is missing/unknown

get.description: Assembles today's collected items into a draft digest issue and sends the owner preview as a best-effort follow-up. Skipped at weekends unless force=true.
Fetches and ranks news from all registered sources. Runs every day, including weekends.
Sends a one-time reminder to subscribers who signed up but never confirmed.
Sends today's digest by email to all active subscribers. Skipped at weekends unless force=true.
Returns a paginated list of all subscribers.
Returns 200 OK when the API is up.

post.description: Fallback for when the live Reddit fetch is blocked (e.g. ScraperAPI 403). Accepts the raw JSON body returned by https://www.reddit.com/r/golang/new.json, transforms it through the standard pipeline, and persists items that fall within the current collection window. De-duplicates on (url, tag), so it is safe to run repeatedly (e.g. on a schedule).

200.description: Items ingested, all duplicates, or none in window

400.description: Unreadable or invalid Reddit JSON payload

post.summary: Manually ingest raw Reddit listing JSON.

get.description: Returns a paginated list of digest issues, optionally filtered by status.

parameters.description: Filter by issue status (e.g. draft, sent)
parameters.name: status

patch.description: Updates the subject and summary of a draft issue. Returns 409 if the issue is not in draft status.

Rewrites the position of each item in the issue using the supplied order — item_ids[i] becomes position i. The submitted ids must exactly match the set of items currently linked to the issue; partial reorders are rejected. Returns the refreshed issue. Returns 409 if the issue is not in draft status.
patch.summary: Reorder digest items within a draft issue.

delete.description: Removes an item from a draft issue by clearing items.issue_id. The item row is preserved in the raw pool and can be re-included by a future build. Returns the refreshed issue. Returns 409 if the issue is not in draft status.
delete.summary: Unlink a digest item from a draft issue.

get.description: Returns a single digest issue, including its items. The {key} path parameter is interpreted as a numeric issue ID if it parses as a positive integer; otherwise it is treated as a slug.
get.summary: Fetch a digest issue by ID or slug.

get.description: Returns a single news item identified by its numeric ID.

400.description: ID is required or not a positive integer

get.summary: Fetch a news item by ID.

get.description: Returns engagement metrics per issue with optional date filtering and sorting.

parameters.description: Relative window: day, week, month, year, all
parameters.name: period

parameters.description: Sort key: click_rate, open_rate, total_clicks, unique_clicks, total_opens, unique_opens, delivered, sent_at
parameters.name: sort

get.description: Returns engagement stats plus the top-clicked links for one issue identified by its date slug.
Returns a time series for one issue's chosen engagement metric, bucketed by day or week. When no window is supplied it defaults to the issue's send date through now.

parameters.description: Relative window: day, week, month, year, all
parameters.name: period

parameters.description: Metric: delivered, unique_opens, total_opens, unique_clicks, total_clicks, open_rate, click_rate
parameters.name: metric

500.description: Failed to fetch issue trend data

get.description: Returns the top-clicked news items enriched with title, tag, and source metadata.

parameters.description: Relative window: day, week, month, year, all
parameters.name: period

get.description: Gathers the last seven days of engagement data (with a week-over-week comparison) and posts a formatted summary to Slack. Scheduled every Friday at 15:00 UTC.
Returns social posts joined with their latest engagement counts (likes, reposts, comments, impressions), optionally filtered by date range.

parameters.description: Relative window: day, week, month, year, all
parameters.name: period

get.description: Returns total clicks aggregated by item source.

parameters.description: Relative window: day, week, month, year, all
parameters.name: period

get.description: Returns subscriber growth and churn bucketed over time.

parameters.description: Relative window: day, week, month, year, all
parameters.name: period

get.description: Returns headline engagement numbers (delivered, opens, clicks, rates) for a period.

parameters.description: Relative window: day, week, month, year, all
parameters.name: period

get.description: Returns total clicks aggregated by item tag.

parameters.description: Relative window: day, week, month, year, all
parameters.name: period

get.description: Returns a time series for a chosen engagement metric, bucketed by day or week.

parameters.description: Relative window: day, week, month, year, all
parameters.name: period

parameters.description: Metric: delivered, unique_opens, total_opens, unique_clicks, total_clicks, open_rate, click_rate
parameters.name: metric

get.description: Returns every row in social_posts with status='draft' (any kind, any platform). The dashboard renders these in the Drafts tab so an operator can review and edit before the 11:00 publish cron.

patch.description: Replaces the text body of a draft social post. Only rows with status='draft' may be edited; the request is rejected with 409 if the row has already been published, errored, or cancelled.

409.description: Row is no longer a draft

post.description: Transitions a draft row to status='cancelled'. The publish cron filters cancelled rows out and rotation idempotency counts them as "already handled", preventing tomorrow's build from regenerating the same subject.

409.description: Row is no longer a draft

post.summary: Cancel a social draft so the publish cron skips it.

get.description: For every social post published in the last 30 days, fetches current engagement counts from the originating platform and upserts them into social_metrics. Scheduled daily at 03:00 UTC.
Publishes the day's featured draft rows (one per configured platform) — recap and other rotation kinds are left untouched so the 15:00 rotation slot picks them up. Skipped at weekends.

Publishes the day's rotation drafts (recap on Monday, community on Wednesday, new_source/spotlight/cta on Friday). Featured drafts are deliberately excluded — they belong to the 11:00 cron. Skipped at weekends.

post.description: Registers an email address for the daily digest and sends a confirmation email.

get.description:
Removes a subscriber using the token from the digest email.
GET redirects to /unsubscribed/; POST implements the RFC 8058 one-click unsubscribe and returns 200 OK.

post.description:
Removes a subscriber using the token from the digest email.
GET redirects to /unsubscribed/; POST implements the RFC 8058 one-click unsubscribe and returns 200 OK.
Receives Resend email events (opens, clicks, bounces). Public, but every request is verified against its Svix-style signature; unsigned or tampered requests are rejected.
